findings.text: Hip measurements use the existing shoulder -> hip -> knee landmark triplets and the unsigned internal vector-angle formula documented in the code.
Large left/right differences, when present, are reported as ranked observations from existing computed values, not as clinical abnormalities or formula errors.

This audit can identify missing or low-confidence landmark evidence, but it cannot prove occlusion, camera perspective, or MediaPipe estimation error without paired visual review and external ground truth.

evidence_notes: No missing/nonfinite hip-triplet landmark evidence detected in existing debug records.

hip_discrepancy_summary.rank_policy: Ranked by existing computed abs(hip_flexion_left - hip_flexion_right); no clinical threshold is applied.

measurement_definitions.convention: Unsigned internal 3D vector angle from MediaPipe landmark coordinates.
measurement_definitions.expected_range_degrees: 0 to 180 when vectors are finite and nonzero; NaN otherwise.
measurement_definitions.interpretation_boundary: MediaPipe-derived geometric approximation; not laboratory inverse kinematics or a clinical score.

No ranked finite hip-pair frame shows missing or nonfinite hip-triplet landmarks in the available records.
No camera calibration, view label, synchronized multi-view data, or external ground-truth video annotation is present in the frame database.
Hip flexion is represented as an unsigned internal shoulder-hip-knee vector angle from MediaPipe landmarks; this is a documented geometric definition, not laboratory inverse kinematics.
candidate_origins.status: documented limitation, not proven frame-specific cause

origin_assessment.evidence_standard: Classification is based only on existing measurements, landmark coordinates, visibility, confidence, and documented angle definitions; no thresholds beyond MediaPipe-style visibility/confidence quality flags are used for clinical interpretation.
origin_assessment.summary: Ranked discrepancy frames contain low visibility or confidence on landmarks used directly in the hip-angle triplets.

prompt_25_scope.notes: This investigation reads existing measurements, landmarks, confidence, visibility, and vector geometry for developer review only.
prompt_25_scope.purpose: Investigate likely origins of observed left/right hip measurement discrepancies using existing Prompt 23/24 diagnostics.

recommendations: Use Measurement Debug Mode to visually inspect ranked frames before considering any formula change.
If recurrent discrepancies coincide with low shoulder, hip, or knee visibility, document the affected frames as measurement-quality concerns rather than changing biomechanics automatically.
Do not alter the hip angle definition without paired validation data or an explicitly approved future scientific prompt.

scope.notes: Angles, deltas, symmetry values, landmarks, confidence, and visibility are read from existing frame records.
scope.purpose: Investigate observed left/right hip measurement differences using existing diagnostic outputs.
